heroHeadline: Recession Odds Reach 60% (JPMorgan); China PMI Returns to Expansion; Trump-Xi Summit Opens in Beijing as Iran Ceasefire Signals Emerge
heroSubtitle: JPMorgan raises US recession probability to 60% — highest of major banks — as Liberation Day nears its one-year mark with $166B in tariff revenue under legal threat. China NBS PMI surged to 50.4 in March (first expansion since March 2025). Markets rallied April 1 on Iran ceasefire signals (Brent ~$98.79, S&P +0.7%, 10Y yield retreating to ~4.30%). Conference Board Expectations Index at 70.9 for 14th consecutive month below recession-warning threshold of 80.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Conference Board, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Yale Budget Lab, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers
